questions.text:
A student has a portable tablet computer. Identify two input devices that could be built into the portable tablet computer.
1
2

marking_points.text: Touch screen
Microphone
Button
Webcam // (digital) camera
Accelerometer
Biometric device

questions.text: A student has a portable tablet computer. Identify one output device that could be built into the portable tablet computer.

marking_points.text: Screen
Speaker
LED

questions.text: A student has a portable tablet computer. Identify one type of storage device that could be built into the portable tablet computer.

marking_points.text: SSD // Solid-state drive // Solid-state (device)

questions.text:
Hypertext markup language (HTML) colour codes can be represented as hexadecimal. Tick (✓) one box to show which statement about the hexadecimal number system is incorrect.
A It uses the values 0 to 9 and A to F.
B It can be used as a shorter representation of binary.
C It is a base 10 system.
D It can be used to represent error codes.

rows: A It uses the values 0 to 9 and A to F.
B It can be used as a shorter representation of binary.
C It is a base 10 system.
D It can be used to represent error codes.

marking_points.text: C

questions.text:
Hypertext markup language (HTML) colour codes can be represented as hexadecimal. Denary numbers can be converted to hexadecimal. Convert the three denary numbers to hexadecimal.
20
32
165

marking_points.text: A5

questions.text: The binary number 10100011 is stored in random access memory (RAM). A logical left shift of three places is performed on the binary number. Give the 8-bit binary number that will be stored after the shift has taken place.

The binary number 10100011 is stored in random access memory (RAM). Tick (✓) one box to show which statement about a logical left shift of two places is correct.
A It would divide the binary number by 2.
B It would multiply the binary number by 2.
C It would divide the binary number by 4.
D It would multiply the binary number by 4.

rows: A It would divide the binary number by 2.
B It would multiply the binary number by 2.
C It would divide the binary number by 4.
D It would multiply the binary number by 4.

marking_points.text: D

questions.text: The binary number 10100011 is stored in random access memory (RAM). 10100011 can be stored as a two's complement integer. Convert the two's complement integer 10100011 to denary. Show all your working.

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for correct working, one mark for correct answer

marking_points.text: One mark for correct working Example: Flip and add
One mark for correct answer: −93

questions.text: The binary number is measured as a byte because it has 8 bits. State how many bytes there are in a kibibyte (KiB).
Data packets are transmitted across a network from one computer to another computer. Describe the structure of a data packet.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: A packet is split into three different sections
… the header
… the payload
… the trailer

questions.text: Data packets are transmitted across a network from one computer to another computer. Packet switching is used to transmit the data packets across the network. Identify the device that controls which path is taken by each data packet.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: Router

questions.text: Data packets are transmitted across a network from one computer to another computer. Serial data transmission is used to transmit the data packets across the network. Explain why serial data transmission is used to transmit the data packets.
questions.topic_name: Types and Methods of Data Transmission

marking_points.text: The network may be spread over a long distance …
… so it is more reliable
Bits will be sent/arrive in sequence
…so bits less likely to be skewed
Less crosstalk/interference
… so less likely to have errors
The data may not need to be transmitted at a fast speed // data transmission speed of serial is adequate
The cables in the network only use serial transmission

questions.text: A computer uses both random access memory (RAM) and secondary storage. State the purpose of secondary storage.

marking_points.text: To store data/files/software permanently

questions.text:
A computer uses both random access memory (RAM) and secondary storage. One type of secondary storage is optical. Circle three examples of optical storage.
read only memory (ROM)  secure digital (SD) card  compact disk (CD)
hard disk drive (HDD)  digital versatile disk (DVD)
Blu-ray disk  universal serial bus (USB) drive  solid-state drive (SSD)

marking_points.text: Compact disk (CD)
Digital versatile disk (DVD)
Blu-ray disk

questions.text: A computer uses both random access memory (RAM) and secondary storage. Explain why a computer needs RAM.

marking_points.text: To store data temporarily
To store data currently in use
To store software currently in use
Data can be accessed faster (than secondary storage)
To speed up the fetch stage (of the fetch-execute cycle)

questions.text: A computer uses both random access memory (RAM) and secondary storage. The computer processes instructions using the fetch–decode–execute (FDE) cycle. Draw and annotate a diagram to show the process of the fetch stage of the FDE cycle.

marking_points.text: The registers MAR, MDR and PC
Address sent from PC to MAR
…using address bus (award anywhere for correct use of address bus)
…PC is incremented
Address sent from MAR to RAM
Data/instruction sent from RAM to MDR
…using data bus (award anywhere for correct use of data bus)
Data/instruction sent from MDR to CIR/CU
Control unit sending control signal for any part of the process
… using control bus (award anywhere for correct use of control bus)

questions.text: A computer needs firmware and system software to operate. State the purpose of firmware.

marking_points.text: Permanently store instructions (in ROM)
Stores instructions to boot up/start up the computer
Provides the operating system with a platform to run on
Controls/manages/allows communication with hardware
Store instructions securely (to stop them being easily corrupted)

questions.text: A computer needs firmware and system software to operate. Give one example of firmware.

marking_points.text: Bootstrap
Bootloader
BIOS
Operating system (in embedded system)
Programs (in embedded systems)

questions.text:
A computer needs firmware and system software to operate. Give two examples of system software.
1
2

marking_points.text: Operating system
Utility software

questions.text:
Data is encrypted to keep it safe during transmission. Complete the paragraph about asymmetric encryption. Use the terms from the list. Some of the terms in the list will not be used. You should only use a term once.
asymmetric  certificate  cipher text
decrypted  encrypted  parallel key  plain text  private key
protected  public key  serial key  symmetric

[blank] is encrypted into [blank] using a [blank] . The encrypted data is then transmitted from the sender to the receiver. The encrypted data is then decrypted using a [blank] .

answers.scoring_rule: One mark for each correct term in the correct place.

marking_points.text: plain text
cipher text
public key
private key

questions.text: A farmer uses an automated robot to plant seeds in the field. State what is meant by the robot being automated.

marking_points.text: The robot can perform actions without human intervention

questions.text:
A farmer uses an automated robot to plant seeds in the field. Give three characteristics of a robot.
1
2
3

marking_points.text: It has a mechanical structure/framework
It has electrical components
It is programmable
It can move

questions.text: A farmer uses an automated robot to plant seeds in the field. The robot plants seeds and stops when it reaches a fence. It then turns and continues planting seeds. The robot uses sensors and a microprocessor to know when it reaches a fence. Explain how the robot uses sensors and a microprocessor to know it has reached a fence.

marking_points.text: A proximity sensor is used
The sensor continuously sends digitised data to the microprocessor
The microprocessor compares the data to a stored value/range of values
If the value is within the range/matches the robot continues planting
If the value is above/below/outside the range a signal is sent by the microprocessor to turn/stop the robot
… using an actuator to turn/stop the robot
This process repeats until the robot is turned off

questions.text:
A farmer uses an automated robot to plant seeds in the field. Give two advantages of the farmer using an automated robot to plant seeds.
1
2

marking_points.text: The robot is more accurate when planting seeds
The robot does not need to take breaks // works 24/7
The robot will not get bored of performing a repetitive task
The farmer can perform other tasks whilst the robot is planting the seeds
The robot may be faster/more efficient (at planting seeds)
Safer as the robot can work in a more dangerous environments (e.g. with animals in a field)
Doesn't need to pay labour costs/wages

questions.text:
A farmer uses an automated robot to plant seeds in the field. Give two disadvantages of the farmer using an automated robot to plant seeds.
1
2

marking_points.text: The robot may have been expensive to purchase
The robot may need maintenance and this may be costly
The robot may take away jobs from humans that previously planted the seeds
Farmer/workers may be deskilled
Farmer may need training in how to use the robot
If it breaks the farmer will need to manually plant
If faulty it may cause damage to farm/seeds
Cannot adapt to a new/unexpected event

questions.text: A farmer uses an automated robot to plant seeds in the field. The robot is adapted to have machine learning capabilities. Explain how this will improve the robot.

marking_points.text: The robot can now adapt itself by changing its own rules/data/ processes
The robot will become more efficient
… as it has a greater knowledge of its surroundings/environment
… as it can remember where a fence is
… as it can remember the route it needs to take
… as it can remember where any obstacles are to avoid
… as it can remember where to start and stop sowing seeds
… as it may make fewer errors

questions.text: A company owner has installed a new network. Data is correct before it is transmitted across the network. The company owner is concerned that data might have errors after transmission. Explain how the data might have errors after transmission.

marking_points.text: Data could be lost
Data could be gained/added
Data could be changed
Bits could be reassembled in the wrong order
Interference could occur
Crosstalk could occur
Data collisions could occur
Data packets could time out/reach their hop count
Network could be infected with malware

questions.text: A company owner has installed a new network. The company owner decides to introduce an error detection system to check the data for errors after transmission. The error detection system uses an odd parity check and a positive automatic repeat query (ARQ). Describe how the error detection system operates to check for errors.

marking_points.text: The 1s are counted (in each byte)
Each byte has a parity bit
If the number of 1s are odd the parity bit is 0 (otherwise it is 1)
(The first packet of) data is sent and a timer is started
The receiving device counts the number of 1s (in each byte)
If the number of 1s are odd/data meets odd parity an acknowledgement is sent to say the data is error free …
… the sender then sends the next packet of data …
… and the timer is restarted
If the number of 1s is even an acknowledgement is not sent
If no acknowledgement is received within a set timeframe/before timeout …
… the data packet is resent

questions.text:
A company owner has installed a new network. The company owner decides to introduce an error detection system to check the data for errors after transmission. Give two other error detection methods that could be used.
1
2

marking_points.text: Echo check
Checksum
Even parity check
Negative ARQ

questions.text: A company owner has installed a new network. The company owner also installs a firewall to help protect the network from hackers and malware. Explain how the firewall operates to help protect the network.

marking_points.text: Criteria can be set (for traffic)
… such as a blacklist/whitelist (of IP addresses)
It will examine traffic coming into the network
It will check that the traffic meets the set criteria
… and will reject it if it does not meet criteria
Certain ports used by hackers can be blocked/closed

questions.text:
A company owner has installed a new network. The company owner also installs a firewall to help protect the network from hackers and malware. Give two examples of malware that the firewall can help protect the network from.
1
2

marking_points.text: Virus
Worm
Trojan horse
Spyware
Adware
Ransomware
